category_title: Students with autism using typed communication and requiring extended processing time
minimal_context: Shaffer is sitting with his classmates during a free period to begin group work.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Shaffer, it is time for group work. Please initiate the conversation questions with your classmates.

STUDENT: (Sits as a silent young man in the front row staring straight ahead, tapping his thumbs together.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start working together with them.

STUDENT: (Types on his communication device) I think it’s so difficult to initiate conversation questions if you don’t have a teacher in the introduction. I look to an adult to really make the bridge.

TUTOR: Just try your best to talk to them on your own.

STUDENT: (Remains silent, staring straight ahead and tapping his thumbs together, experiencing the distances of inconsolation of the sad feelings because the teacher did not act as a bridge.)

options.A: Never offer personal opinions or draw conclusions, and do not interpret the classmates' contributions.
options.B: Ask one of Shaffer's classmates about working with him during a free period.
options.C: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the group work content during free periods.
options.D: Collaboratively identify social-emotional targeted goals with clearly defined definitions of desirable and undesirable behaviors.
